South Africa and several other southern African nations, alongside Australia, will be home to the worldâs largest radio telescope, the Square Kilometer Array, the first stage of which is set for completion in 2027. That, as well as the continentâs growing scientific prowess, led to the decision to host the assembly there.

âThis is a significant milestone,â one African astronomer wrote in The Conversation: Space science in Africa âhas grown and strengthened tremendously over the past decade.â
